goal: Audit and improve this repo’s documentation and AGENTS.md guidance so future Codex sessions have better context and stop less often; inspect repo first, derive commands/conventions from repo, change docs only, use a new worktree, verify where possible, and list blockers/unknowns.

summary.title: Audit and improve Codex-facing repository guidance
summary.what: Expanded AGENTS.md with concise repository-specific Codex guidance and updated CONTRIBUTING.md with the local setup/build/test/lint/hygiene command reference, formatting/typecheck unknowns, generated-file constraints, testing guidance, and done criteria. The Mainline policy pointer remains intact and in sync; application behavior was not changed.
summary.why: Future Codex sessions were stopping for missing repo context. The new guidance derives commands and constraints from Makefile, CI workflows, scripts, README, CONTRIBUTING, .gitignore, hygiene checks, and current Mainline guidance, matching OpenAI Codex best-practice advice to keep durable, practical repo guidance in AGENTS.md.
summary.user_goal: Read OpenAI Codex best practices first, inspect this repo before editing, improve documentation and AGENTS.md guidance in a new worktree, document discoverable commands/conventions/generated files/done criteria/unknowns, avoid behavior changes, run verification, and report files changed/results/blockers/follow-up.

decisions.chose: Add a concise repo-specific section to AGENTS.md outside the marker-bounded Mainline agent guidance and keep detailed command elaboration in CONTRIBUTING.md.
decisions.rationale: AGENTS.md is automatically loaded for future Codex sessions, while CONTRIBUTING.md is the existing human-facing development guide. Keeping the Mainline pointer intact avoids duplicating the full Mainline workflow in repo guidance.

decisions.chose: Document only commands found in Makefile, CI, scripts, README, and CONTRIBUTING; explicitly list missing format/typecheck targets as unknowns.
decisions.rationale: The user asked not to invent commands or conventions. The docs now distinguish discoverable commands from standard Go formatting/typecheck implications.

decisions.chose: Use docs-focused checks plus Mainline agent-guidance status rather than forcing full Go gates for docs-only changes.
decisions.rationale: The changed files are documentation only. git diff whitespace checks, hygiene scanning, and mainline agents check directly cover the edited surfaces; a broader quick-test attempt was separately captured as blocked because it did not finish promptly on the clean worktree.

rejected.alternative: Copy the full Mainline start/append/seal workflow into AGENTS.md.
rejected.reason: Historical Mainline guidance says the full workflow belongs in the skill; duplicating it in AGENTS.md, hooks, and skill would drift.

rejected.alternative: Update README.md/README.zh.md development sections as part of this docs audit.
rejected.reason: README already contains a development command section and broader product docs; CONTRIBUTING.md was the smaller relevant setup/test doc needing the command matrix.

rejected.alternative: Invent make format or make typecheck commands for completeness.
rejected.reason: No such targets exist in Makefile or CI, so the docs explicitly call them unknown and map typechecking to build/test/lint.

acknowledged_constraints.note: Kept the full Mainline workflow in the skill and used AGENTS.md only for a concise policy pointer plus repo-specific Codex context.

review_notes: Docs-only change. Verification run: git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD, make hygiene, mainline agents check --json, and mainline preflight --json after commit.

Baseline make quick-test was attempted before editing but was terminated after it produced early package passes and then made no further output for roughly three minutes; it is reported as a blocker, not as a pass.

architectural_claims: AGENTS.md is the repo-local entrypoint for Codex-specific context, but the complete Mainline workflow remains owned by skills/mainline/SKILL.md and the installed skill.
Makefile and GitHub Actions workflows are the command source of truth for setup/build/test/lint/hygiene documentation.
Generated and local-only artifacts are documented as avoid-edit surfaces for future agents.
security_implications: No runtime security behavior changed; AGENTS.md and CONTRIBUTING.md now point future agents at hygiene checks and local/generated files that must not be committed.
migration_notes: No migration required; documentation-only change on branch work/docs-agent-guidance-audit.
